steps.title: Spot the pattern the student can emphasize
steps.content: The defining signal: two critics (The Economist and O Magazine) both emphasize storytelling and narrative quality, while the Missoulian emphasizes something different (monumental achievement). This reveals a shared strength the student can highlight.

steps.title: Apply this pattern to the choices
steps.content:
C does this by opening with “Critics praised Treuer's book for its compelling narrative” to unify the shared element, then providing two specific examples—both highlighting storytelling—with “likewise” to signal the parallel.
  • A mentions the book was widely reviewed without capturing the similarity
  • B uses only one critic's view
  • D explicitly contrasts how critics differed
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
